subject: Computer Networking
title: IMAP vs. POP3: Retrieving Email
description: A fast breakdown of IMAP vs. POP3: Retrieving Email for builders.

hook.text: You read an email on your phone. Open your laptop — it's there too. IMAP keeps everything in sync.

buildup.text: IMAP stores mail on the server. Every device sees the same inbox, folders, and read status.

discovery.text: POP3 downloads mail to one device and often deletes it from the server. Simple but not multi-device friendly.

twist.text: POP3 was fine when people had one computer. Today, with phones and tablets, IMAP is the clear winner.

climax.text: Most modern email services default to IMAP. POP3 still exists but is rarely the recommended choice.

punchline.text: IMAP syncs. POP3 downloads. The difference matters.

quiz.question: Why is IMAP preferred over POP3 for modern email?
options: It keeps mail on the server so all devices stay in sync
It downloads mail faster than POP3
